Params Editor is an extension built to simplify how developers, QA testers, & technical professionals work with URL query params. It intelligently detects param types and provides dedicated input controls for each - from simple text to complex JSON objects.

Reviews [...] As a developer, I frequently found myself wasting time manually editing URL parameters during development and testing. This process was not only tedious but also error-prone. While existing tools offered basic URL editing capabilities, they lacked specialized inputs for different data types, making them only marginally better than directly modifying URLs in the address bar. This was particularly frustrating when working with complex parameters. Params Editor was born from this frustration. I created it to provide a more intelligent approach to URL parameter manipulation, with a strong focus on developer productivity and a polished user experience. By automatically detecting parameter types and providing appropriate input controls, it eliminates the common pain points developers face when

Google's Gemma 4 looks like a serious leap forward in open AI models.

An open model family built for advanced reasoning and agentic workflows, it solves a key problem: getting frontier-level intelligence without massive compute costs or closed ecosystems.

Stands out for its intelligence-per-parameter — outperforming models up to 20x larger while running efficiently on phones, laptops, and desktops.

results.title: Claude Opus 4.7 Reviews (2026) | Product Hunt
results.content:
For complex agentic coding, you need more than just fast autocomplete or massive parameter counts; you need deep, multi-step architectural reasoning. While GPT-5 and Gemini 2.5 are incredibly fast and highly capable, Opus 4.7 feels like a true senior staff engineer. When my autonomous agents need to plan a complex data pipeline, debug a tricky Python traceback, or write multi-file scripts that will eventually be executed safely inside my Vela micro-VM sandbox, Opus 4.7 consistently maps out the logic without losing the thread. Its ability to hold massive context, reason through edge cases, and self-correct before outputting the final code makes it the undisputed choice for heavy-lifting agent tasks. The other models are great for sprints, but Opus 4.7 is what I use when the architecture [...] What makes it fantastic is that it changes how you work. You are no longer only asking the model to write small functions or boilerplate. You can ask it to plan and execute larger engineering tasks. For senior engineers, this shifts the work from writing every line of code to setting direction, defining guardrails, reviewing outputs, and protecting critical areas like authentication, permissions, JWT flows, and security-sensitive logic.

It feels like another step toward the agentic software development workflow.

Ratings

Ease of use

Reliability

Value for money

Customization

findloc.ai 

used Claude Opus 4.7 to build findloc.ai

•3 reviews

Built findloc.ai end-to-end with Claude Opus 4.7 over a week sprint. What made it different from GPT-4o and Gemini in my side-by-side testing: it pushes back. When my intuition was wrong (I swore the map radius rings looked too big), it measured them against MapLibre's scale bar to prove me wrong instead of just agreeing. That "verify before answering" reflex saved me from a dozen wrong fixes.

Review Runway? [...] Upload your job description + up to 50 resumes, then customize the ranking parameters that actually predict success at your organization. Whether you're: - Netflix looking for engineers who thrive in high-performance culture - A startup needing scrappy problem-solvers who can build from scratch - Oracle needing sales reps who can navigate complex enterprise deals - Coca-Cola wanting consumer brand experts who understand retail The result: Applicant rankings that reflect what success looks like at YOUR company, not generic job categories. Try it free and see how company-specific screening changes your hire quality! What makes a great hire at your company different from your competitors? Drop your unique hiring criteria in the comments - we love hearing what makes each company special! [...] Subscribe

Review Runway?

Runway is praised for its AI-driven applicant ranking tool that streamlines the hiring process by adapting to specific company needs. Users highlight its ability to save time and improve hire quality by accurately predicting candidate fit. The platform is noted for its user-friendly interface and effectiveness in connecting startups with skilled talent. Feedback from users emphasizes the tool's efficiency in reducing screening time and enhancing recruitment precision, making it a valuable asset for modern recruiters and startups seeking meaningful collaborations.

results.content:
future. I look forward to this. My only wish is that the service would use storage limits rather than a limit of the number of documents uploaded. I have about 4,000 small research documents from my team I'd love to upload, but am limited to 1000 docs at a time. I would also like to see a more robust file management capability. Loading documents into folders, and allowing folders to be deleted, added and removed from agents, etc. I am sure those more robust features will come. In addition, I'd like to have the ability to refine the document ingestion parameters, including the ability to set some meta data. [...] #### What's great

Report

 29 views

•2 reviews [...] Report

 29 views

•2 reviews

To have the ability to learn, analyze, and interact with a document on any topic just blew me away. From multiple choice answers, true or false, Q & A creation of the most common pitfalls or best advice for the end consumer, the list goes on and on. The ability to literally train your own chatbot on a particular topic and have it available at the click of a mouse is to me incredible. I am using it for content on the subject of my interest and for education as well. I feel the more I interact, the more and better ideas keep flowing with the different applications. The flexibility to read beyond PDF and into Word, Excel, and PPP gives this app a huge advantage over competitors. I am glad I found it. This is a keeper.

#### What's great
